summary: Joel shares a brief reflection on the emotional reward of building shrinkwrap (packaged) software. He expresses that the best part of software development is seeing real people use your product to accomplish meaningful work. He then quotes a user testimonial praising CityDesk, Fog Creek's content management product, as superior to competing tools in power, flexibility, and ease of use.
key_points: The greatest reward in software development is seeing users accomplish real things with your product
Shrinkwrap software provides a unique satisfaction because it reaches end users directly
User testimonials serve as validation that a product delivers genuine value
money_quotes: The best part of developing shrinkwrap software is the wonderful feeling you get when people actually use your product, the work of your mind and hands, and get great things done.
key_insight: The deepest satisfaction in building software comes not from the code itself but from watching real users accomplish meaningful work with what you've created.
